title: Morning Drive: the songs that got us through winter
content: A round-up of the most-requested tracks on Rhythm FM 94.7 Morning Drive this July. Thank you Joburg, you have taste.
excerpt: The winter playlist you built with us.

title: Why I'm making a podcast (and why it's off the record)
content: Fifteen years behind a radio desk taught me that the best conversations happen after the mic goes down. Off-Mic is my attempt to catch those. More soon.
excerpt: A note on why Off-Mic exists.

title: Voices of the City: season 2 recap
content: Looking back at a season of street-level stories from Johannesburg. Season 3 is already in the works.
excerpt: A recap of Voices of the City season 2.

title: Rhythm FM turns 30 this year
content: Three decades on air. We're producing a special anniversary documentary featuring voices from across the station's history.

title: How we record: inside Bassline Studio
content: A quick tour of the Melville studio where the Off-Mic pilot came together, and why room sound matters more than gear.
excerpt: A look inside our recording space.

title: Draft: Off-Mic season 1 guest wishlist
content: Early notes on dream guests for the first full season. Not for publication - internal planning only.

title: Rhythm FM community roadshow dates
content: We're bringing the station to the suburbs this spring. Full list of pop-up broadcast locations across Gauteng.
excerpt: Where to find us on the road.

title: Draft: podcasting on a shoestring - lessons so far
content: Notes on producing an independent show without a big budget. Draft - still gathering numbers.

title: Draft: Off-Mic Pilot Ep 1 - The Ones Who Stayed (show notes)
content: SHOW NOTES - Off-Mic Pilot Episode 1: 'The Ones Who Stayed'. Guest: Palesa Mokoena. Runtime: 42:15. Recorded at Rhythm FM Studio B, Melville; mixed by Naledi Tau. Segments: cold open, main conversation, reflection break at 30:00, outro. Publish date TBC - holding until written release is signed.
excerpt: Off-Mic pilot episode 1 show notes - guest Palesa Mokoena, runtime 42:15.
